62f072b47434cffff7b5a0d2.leadin: Will Eisner’s graphic novel adaptation of this epic begins with its antagonist being granted magical powers from the Great Grey Rock of Evil. For 10 points each:
62f072b47434cffff7b5a0d2.leadin_sanitized: Will Eisner's graphic novel adaptation of this epic begins with its antagonist being granted magical powers from the Great Grey Rock of Evil. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this epic about a crippled child who goes on to kill the Sosso king Soumaoro and found the Mali Empire. It is one of many tales orally preserved by bards known as griots (“GREE-ohs”).
Many stories in West African oral tradition are about this trickster god who typically took the form of a spider.

Yoruban (“YO-roo-bin”) oral tradition inspired much of this author’s work, such as a novel about a boy abandoned in a supernatural realm titled My Life in the Bush of Ghosts. In this author’s first novel, a rich man travels to Dead’s Town.
parts_sanitized: Name this epic about a crippled child who goes on to kill the Sosso king Soumaoro and found the Mali Empire. It is one of many tales orally preserved by bards known as griots ("GREE-ohs").
Many stories in West African oral tradition are about this trickster god who typically took the form of a spider.

Yoruban ("YO-roo-bin") oral tradition inspired much of this author's work, such as a novel about a boy abandoned in a supernatural realm titled My Life in the Bush of Ghosts. In this author's first novel, a rich man travels to Dead's Town.
answers: Epic of Sundiata [or Sundiata: An Epic of Old Mali; or anything indicating an epic about Sundiata; accept Sunjata, Son-Jara, or Mari Diata in place of “Sundiata”]
Amos Tutuola (The unnamed novel is The Palm-Wine Drinkard.)
